records.ety: [F. aptitude, LL. aptitudo, fr. L. aptus. See Apt, and cf. Attitude.]

defs: A natural or acquired disposition or capacity for a particular purpose, or tendency to a particular action or effect; as, oil has an aptitude to burn.
quotes: He seems to have had a peculiar aptitude for the management of irregular troops.

defs: A general fitness or suitableness; adaptation.
quotes: That sociable and helpful aptitude which God implanted between man and woman.
He was a boy of remarkable aptitude.
